page: Amazon Web Services m6id.24xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor, delivering 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system includes 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is bundled with 5700 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a 38 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core CPU capabilities, with the stress-ng multi-core test and PassMark CPU score placing in the top 25 percent. Memory bandwidth performance is also strong across both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks. Database and web serving operations show high efficiency, as evidenced by strong results in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance, single-threaded compression, and large-scale LLM text generation tasks, such as inference on a 70B model, fall into average or weak performance tiers.

Given its high resource density, the m6id.24xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring a balance of compute, memory, and high-speed local storage. The inclusion of 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage reduces the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput tasks. This hardware profile is well-suited for database operations, software compilation, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.24xlarge is a general-purpose server utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor offering 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and a 3.5 GHz clock speed. The server features 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory and is bundled with 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU execution, high memory bandwidth, and efficient database operations, though single-core and large LLM inference tasks are more limited. This configuration is suitable for databases, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.24xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for database and multi-threaded workloads.

tagline: A general-purpose server featuring ninety-six virtual cores, balanced memory, and local NVMe SSD storage for multi-threaded and database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor
384.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 4.0 GB per core
5700 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Strong performance in multi-core benchmarks, database operations, and web serving
Suitable for databases, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing
